name: McKay Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: the graph G and its complete invariant under all edge operations

VINIT.justification: void state before any graph structure exists; the genesis of the recursive computation

TANCH.chosen_element: complete graph K_n or the terminal coloring state
TANCH.justification: the closed boundary that contains all possible colorings; the maximal chromatic polynomial evaluation

AFWD.justification: forward morphism reducing graph complexity; directed transformation toward simpler structure

AREV.chosen_element: edge deletion (e → remove edge, keep vertices)
AREV.justification: reverse/descent morphism; contravariant operation that undoes contraction semantically

CLINK.justification: sequential chaining of morphisms; the core McKay recurrence P(G,k) = P(G-e,k) - P(G/e,k)

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the chromatic polynomial P(G,k) itself as self-referential identity
IMSCRIB.justification: self-recognition: the polynomial is invariant under isomorphic relabeling; it knows itself

FSPLIT.chosen_element: deletion-contraction fork: G splits into {G-e, G/e}
FSPLIT.justification: one graph branches into two distinct paths: deletion (keep edge endpoints separate) and contraction (merge them)

FFUSE.chosen_element: recombination via the McKay recurrence relation
FFUSE.justification: branches reconstitute the original chromatic polynomial: P(G,k) = P(G-e,k) - P(G/e,k) recovers G's invariant

EVALT.chosen_element: successful base case: single vertex or empty graph
EVALT.justification: affirmative branch; positive evaluation where recursion terminates with known chromatic polynomial

EVALF.chosen_element: failed or degenerate case: disconnected components or multi-edge loops
EVALF.justification: negative branch; cases requiring special handling or returning zero contribution

ENGAGR.chosen_element: isomorphic graphs with identical chromatic polynomials
ENGAGR.justification: paradice: two distinct graph structures held simultaneously as equivalent under the invariant; both true without resolution

IFIX.chosen_element: computed chromatic polynomial value P(G,k) recorded in lookup table
IFIX.justification: permanent append-only record; once computed, the value is fixed and cannot be undone; memoization

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: edge e in graph G
phase_2.split_input: chromatic polynomial P(G,k) of graph G
split_outputs: P(G-e,k): chromatic polynomial of G with edge e deleted
P(G/e,k): chromatic polynomial of G with edge e contracted
phase_2.fuse_result: P(G,k) = P(G-e,k) - P(G/e,k) recovers the original chromatic polynomial exactly

phase_3.void_description: 00 — no graph, no coloring, computation not yet initiated; the empty seed state
phase_3.true_description: 01 — base case reached (single vertex or empty graph); chromatic polynomial known and affirmative
phase_3.false_description: 10 — degenerate case (disconnected, multi-edge, or loop); contribution is zero or requires special handling
phase_3.both_description: 11 — isomorphic non-identical graphs with the same chromatic polynomial; two distinct structures held as equivalent

steps.domain_action: initialize with empty graph ∅; no vertices, no edges, no coloring yet
establish boundary as the complete graph K_n or the terminal chromatic polynomial state
recognize P(G,k) as self-referential identity; the polynomial is invariant under graph isomorphism
select an edge e ∈ E(G) and fork into two branches: deletion and contraction
forward morphism on T-arm (deletion branch): remove edge e, keep both endpoints as separate vertices
evaluate T-arm: if G-e is a base case (single vertex or empty), return known P(G-e,k)
compose recursively on T-arm: if G-e is not base case, apply FSPLIT again to G-e
reverse morphism on F-arm (contraction branch): merge endpoints of edge e into a single vertex
evaluate F-arm: if G/e is a base case, return known P(G/e,k)
compose recursively on F-arm: if G/e is not base case, apply FSPLIT again to G/e
recombine both branches via McKay recurrence: P(G,k) = P(G-e,k) - P(G/e,k)
record computed P(G,k) in memoization table; permanent, append-only, cannot be undone
recognize that non-isomorphic graphs may yield identical chromatic polynomials; both held simultaneously
verify self-consistency: P(G,k) is independent of edge selection; any edge e yields the same result
chain all deletion-contraction steps into a single compositional witness of the theorem
close the boundary: all possible colorings of G are now enumerated and verified by P(G,k)

phase_5.compiler_frontend: recursive deletion-contraction algorithm; translates graph structure into chromatic polynomial computation
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: memoization table shared between deletion and contraction branches; both arms communicate via P(G-e,k) and P(G/e,k) values
phase_5.memory_mechanism: lookup table storing computed P(G,k) for each subgraph; state retrieved by graph isomorphism class
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: depth-first traversal of deletion-contraction tree; base cases evaluated first, results propagated upward
phase_5.alfs_store: the McKay recurrence relation P(G,k) = P(G-e,k) - P(G/e,k); canonical bootstrap for all chromatic polynomial computation

phase_6.cycle_cost: one edge operation (deletion or contraction) per recursion step; O(|E|) operations total
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: graph G with |V| vertices and |E| edges; chromatic polynomial unknown
phase_6.post_cycle_state: chromatic polynomial P(G,k) fully computed and memoized; graph structure preserved in isomorphism class
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the deletion-contraction recurrence is a bijective decomposition; no information is lost or created, only reorganized into polynomial form

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → AFWD → EVALT → CLINK → AREV → EVALF → CLINK → FFUSE → IFIX → ENGAGR → IMSCRIB → CLINK → TANCH
-- Class: McKay Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(10,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=16
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑠  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑡 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [6] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [9] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑳  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑭 → 𐑠  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [13] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑳 → 𐑱  | identity — self-imscription
--   [14] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑠 → 𐑡  | composition — regime coherence
--   [15] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑱 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def mckay_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (initialize with empty graph ∅; no vertices, no edges, no coloring yet)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (establish boundary as the complete graph K_n or the terminal chromatic polyno...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑡 𐑚)  -- [2] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (recognize P(G,k) as self-referential identity; the polynomial is invariant un...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (select an edge e ∈ E(G) and fork into two branches: deletion and contraction) / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (forward morphism on T-arm (deletion branch): remove edge e, keep both endpoin...)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (evaluate T-arm: if G-e is a base case (single vertex or empty), return known ...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose recursively on T-arm: if G-e is not base case, apply FSPLIT again to G-e)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism on F-arm (contraction branch): merge endpoints of edge e int...)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (evaluate F-arm: if G/e is a base case, return known P(G/e,k))
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose recursively on F-arm: if G/e is not base case, apply FSPLIT again to G/e))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (recombine both branches via McKay recurrence: P(G,k) = P(G-e,k) - P(G/e,k))
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record computed P(G,k) in memoization table; permanent, append-only, cannot b...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑭 𐑠)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (recognize that non-isomorphic graphs may yield identical chromatic polynomial...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑳 𐑱)  -- [13] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (verify self-consistency: P(G,k) is independent of edge selection; any edge e ...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [14] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (chain all deletion-contraction steps into a single compositional witness of t...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [15] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (close the boundary: all possible colorings of G are now enumerated and verifi...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def mckay_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (mckay_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def mckay_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (mckay_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem mckay_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
